report:
# Review (3 clusters)
- ⚪ **[LOW]** \[test-coverage\] Carrying-task hint is only partially asserted — `agent-sdlc/checker/rules.test.mjs:345` · 1/2 models · _by:_ lens-spec _(test-coverage)_
  AC-2 requires the reviewer-checked finding to point at the carrying-task fix, including that the carrying task produces the artifact the reviewer checks. The test only checks for \`reviewer-checked\` and \`\*Advances:\*\`, so a vague message missing the carrying-task/artifact guidance could still pass.
  _Fix:_ Assert the reviewer-checked finding contains the carrying-task wording and the artifact-producing-task guidance, or compare against the expected hint text.
- ⚪ **[LOW]** \[test-coverage\] Type-unknown AC hint behavior is untested — `agent-sdlc/checker/rules.test.mjs:364` · 1/2 models · _by:_ lens-spec _(test-coverage)_
  AC-2 explicitly requires type-unknown unreached ACs to keep the existing base coverage-forward message, but the tests only cover reviewer-checked and test-backed ACs. A regression that appended the reviewer-checked hint to typeless/null ACs would not be caught.
  _Fix:_ Add a coverage-forward fixture with an AC whose block states no verification type and assert its message remains the base message with no carrying-task hint.
- ⚪ **[LOW]** \[correctness\] Amendment marker example is not valid checker provenance — `agent-sdlc/skills/build/reference/plan-amendments.md:38` · 1/2 models · _by:_ holistic _(correctness)_
  The new protocol says to stamp amendments with \`\<!-- source: mid-build amendment (\<why\>) · \<date\> --\>\`, but the existing provenance parser only recognizes dates after an \`ingested\` token. If an agent follows this as a section-level provenance marker, the checker will treat it as malformed; if it is intended only as mid-section documentary prose, the doc overstates it as a normal provenance marker.
  _Fix:_ Either change the example here and the matching plan SKILL note to \`\<!-- source: mid-build amendment (\<why\>) · ingested YYYY-MM-DD --\>\`, or explicitly state that mid-Plan amendment stamps are documentary and not parsed by the current provenance-marker rule.
prComment:
## Review Gate — Round 1
✅ **PASS** — no blocking findings.

Findings: 3 total — 0 critical · 0 high · 0 medium · 3 low · 0 info.
_Reviewed by:_ holistic + lens-spec · models: codex:gpt-5.5, claude:claude-opus-4-8

### Advisory (non-blocking)
- ⚪ **[LOW]** \[test-coverage\] Carrying-task hint is only partially asserted — `agent-sdlc/checker/rules.test.mjs:345` · 1/2 models · _by:_ lens-spec _(test-coverage)_
  AC-2 requires the reviewer-checked finding to point at the carrying-task fix, including that the carrying task produces the artifact the reviewer checks. The test only checks for \`reviewer-checked\` and \`\*Advances:\*\`, so a vague message missing the carrying-task/artifact guidance could still pass.
  _Fix:_ Assert the reviewer-checked finding contains the carrying-task wording and the artifact-producing-task guidance, or compare against the expected hint text.
- ⚪ **[LOW]** \[test-coverage\] Type-unknown AC hint behavior is untested — `agent-sdlc/checker/rules.test.mjs:364` · 1/2 models · _by:_ lens-spec _(test-coverage)_
  AC-2 explicitly requires type-unknown unreached ACs to keep the existing base coverage-forward message, but the tests only cover reviewer-checked and test-backed ACs. A regression that appended the reviewer-checked hint to typeless/null ACs would not be caught.
  _Fix:_ Add a coverage-forward fixture with an AC whose block states no verification type and assert its message remains the base message with no carrying-task hint.
- ⚪ **[LOW]** \[correctness\] Amendment marker example is not valid checker provenance — `agent-sdlc/skills/build/reference/plan-amendments.md:38` · 1/2 models · _by:_ holistic _(correctness)_
  The new protocol says to stamp amendments with \`\<!-- source: mid-build amendment (\<why\>) · \<date\> --\>\`, but the existing provenance parser only recognizes dates after an \`ingested\` token. If an agent follows this as a section-level provenance marker, the checker will treat it as malformed; if it is intended only as mid-section documentary prose, the doc overstates it as a normal provenance marker.
  _Fix:_ Either change the example here and the matching plan SKILL note to \`\<!-- source: mid-build amendment (\<why\>) · ingested YYYY-MM-DD --\>\`, or explicitly state that mid-Plan amendment stamps are documentary and not parsed by the current provenance-marker rule.
